title: Vriesea pedicellata
intro: Vriesea pedicellata, also known as the 'Lilac Plant' or 'Purple Bromeliad', is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family, characterized by its vibrant lilac-colored leaves and distinctive rosette shape. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, wounds, and skin irritations. Bioactive constituents of Vriesea pedicellata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and extracts, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Vriesea pedicellata is classified within the family Bromeliaceae and the genus Vriesea. It is a species of flowering plant endemic to South America. The plant is further classified as a part of the order Poales. Vriesea pedicellata is closely related to other bromeliad species. The exact classification at the species level may be subject to ongoing scientific research and revision.
